name: Uno Platform
description: Open-source cross-platform framework for building native Mobile, Web, Desktop and Embedded apps with C# and XAML from a single codebase

description.value: Build cross-platform native apps with C#, XAML and WebAssembly

initial-release.comment: Open-sourced in May 2018; developed internally by nventive from 2013-2018

active-maintenance.comment: Very active development with frequent releases; 300+ contributors

reactivity-model.comment: Uses XAML data binding with INotifyPropertyChanged; MVUX adds reactive feeds/states

component-model.comment: XAML-based components using C# partial classes; similar to WinUI/UWP

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in MVVM support plus MVUX (Model-View-Update-eXtended) with reactive feeds and states

opinionatedness.comment: Follows WinUI/UWP conventions; opinionated XAML-based architecture with established .NET patterns

backward-compatibility.comment: Maintains API compatibility with WinUI/UWP; provides migration guides between versions

primary-language.comment: C# is the primary language (98.7% of codebase)

compiles-to.comment: WebAssembly for web target; also compiles to native for mobile/desktop

typescript-support.comment: C# is the primary language; TypeScript not applicable

template-syntax.comment: Uses XAML, Microsoft's declarative UI markup language

styling-approach.comment: XAML Styles and Resources; supports scoped and global styles in XAML format

build-required.comment: Requires .NET SDK and build process; uses MSBuild

wasm-interop.comment: JavaScript interop through WebAssembly; uses .NET's JS interop mechanisms

reactive-primitives.comment: MVUX provides IFeed and IState reactive primitives for state management

computed-values.comment: MVUX feeds support derived/computed values that auto-update

two-way-binding.comment: Full XAML two-way data binding with {x:Bind Mode=TwoWay}

event-handling.comment: XAML event handlers and command bindings

conditional-rendering.comment: Visibility bindings, DataTriggers, and converters for conditional display

list-rendering.comment: ItemsControl, ListView, GridView for collection rendering

component-slots.comment: ContentPresenter and ContentControl for content projection

teleport-portal.comment: No built-in portal/teleport mechanism; popups and dialogs work differently in XAML

suspense.comment: FeedView control handles loading, error, and no-data states for async operations

error-boundaries.comment: No built-in error boundary components; error handling done via try/catch and global handlers

context-provide-inject.comment: Dependency injection via Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection; Resources for XAML-level DI

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: JavaScript interop allows direct DOM access when needed

nested-routes.comment: Nested property in route definitions creates parent-child relationships

dynamic-routes.comment: Route parameters and data passing supported

route-guards.comment: DependsOn and navigation interception for guarded routes

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Supports lazy loading of assemblies and views

built-in-state.comment: Built-in component state via MVVM or MVUX patterns

built-in-global-state.comment: MVUX provides app-wide state management with feeds and states

global-state-library.comment: Model-View-Update-eXtended pattern with IFeed and IState

devtools-state-inspection.comment: No browser devtools extension; debugging done through Visual Studio/VS Code

state-persistence.comment: Requires manual implementation; can use standard .NET storage APIs

time-travel-debugging.comment: Not available as a built-in feature

official-cli.comment: dotnet CLI templates and uno-check tool for environment verification

hot-module-replacement.comment: Hot Reload for C# and XAML supported in Visual Studio, VS Code, and Rider

browser-devtools.comment: No browser extension; uses IDE debugging (Visual Studio, VS Code, Rider)

vs-code-extension.comment: Official Uno Platform extension with Hot Reload, IntelliSense, and debugging support

error-messages.comment: Error messages are generally helpful with .NET stack traces; XAML binding errors can be verbose

documentation-quality.comment: Good documentation with tutorials, API reference, and samples; comprehensive but can be overwhelming

playground.comment: Official Uno Playground built with WASM

bundle-size-core.comment: ~5MB+ minimum with .NET runtime; can be reduced with XAML trimming and Brotli compression

bundle-size-full.comment: ~22MB typical with Brotli compression, IL Linker and XAML trimming; varies significantly by features

virtual-dom.comment: Uses Skia rendering or native controls; no virtual DOM diffing

compile-time-optimization.comment: AOT compilation, Profile Guided AOT (PG-AOT), and XAML trimming available

tree-shaking.comment: IL Linker and XAML Resources Trimming can reduce size by 50%

lazy-components.comment: Supports lazy loading of views and assemblies

ssr-capable.comment: Uno Platform WASM is client-side only; no built-in SSR for web target

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Not included in js-framework-benchmark as it's a .NET/WASM framework, not a traditional JavaScript framework

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Uno Platform uses NuGet, not npm. 130+ million total NuGet downloads across packages

component-libraries.comment: Windows Community Toolkit, Uno.Toolkit, Syncfusion, Infragistics, DevExpress support Uno

meta-framework.comment: Uno Platform itself is the full cross-platform solution; no separate meta-framework

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Developed and maintained by nventive; open source since 2018

job-market-demand.comment: Niche within .NET ecosystem; jobs exist but rare compared to mainstream frameworks

official-testing-library.comment: Uno.UITest for UI testing and Uno.UI.RuntimeTests for unit testing

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Uno.UITest provides unified E2E testing across platforms

snapshot-testing.comment: Supports visual snapshot verification in UI tests

mobile-framework.comment: Native iOS and Android support from same codebase

pwa-support.comment: Built-in PWA manifest support with service worker for WASM target

desktop-framework.comment: Native Windows, macOS, and Linux desktop support from same codebase

learning-curve.comment: Requires knowledge of C#, XAML, .NET, and WASM concepts; familiar for WinUI/WPF developers but steep for others

concepts-to-learn.comment: C#, XAML, data binding, MVVM/MVUX, dependency injection, navigation, styling, resources, controls, layouts, async patterns, platform specifics, builds, deployment

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~10 minutes with .NET SDK and IDE setup; uno-check verifies environment

migration-path.comment: Migration guides from WPF, UWP, Silverlight, and Xamarin.Forms

incremental-adoption.comment: Typically requires full application migration; not designed for incremental adoption in existing JS apps
